nodes.label: Tests for token-aware chunking and parallel chunk execution in graphify.llm.
Force the chars/4 fallback so packing math is deterministic regardless     of w
Many small files should land in a single chunk, not one chunk per file.
When the next file would push the chunk past the budget, start a new chunk.
Files in the same directory should land in the same chunk when they fit.
A file larger than the budget can't be split — it goes alone in a chunk.
When tiktoken is installed, the estimator should call into it for     accurate
Without tiktoken installed, the estimator falls back to chars/4.
Build a deterministic fake extraction result for a chunk.
With max_concurrency > 1, total wall time should be ~max(chunk times),     not
max_concurrency=1 should run sequentially (no thread pool).
A single chunk raising should be logged but not abort the run.     Other chunks
token_budget=None should fall back to legacy fixed-count chunking.
With the default token_budget, many tiny files pack into one chunk.
Build a stub extraction result with a controllable finish_reason.
No retry when finish_reason='stop' — single call, result passes through.
finish_reason='length' triggers split-in-half. Both halves succeed     on the s
When even the half-chunk truncates, split again. With 8 files and a     truncat
If everything truncates, retries stop at max_depth — partial result     kept wi
A single file that truncates can't be split further — surface a     warning and
End-to-end: extract_corpus_parallel routes through adaptive retry,     so a chu
